note: Reference only. Projects whose own docs welcome AI-agent contributions, curated as farm targets for a human choosing where to point crg-farm. Nothing in the harness reads this. evidenceType distinguishes an EXPLICIT written welcome ('AI-assisted contributions are welcome') from a repo that merely ships an agent-instruction file (AGENTS.md / copilot-instructions.md) — the file is a strong signal the maintainers use agents, but it is NOT consent (libsdl-org/SDL ships an AGENTS.md that BANS AI PRs). Verify a repo's live stance before farming, and check the registries + avoid list below. Most explicit-welcome repos REQUIRE an AI-assisted disclosure trailer on the PR — honor it per target.

registries.name: melissawm/open-source-ai-contribution-policies
registries.purpose: source of truth — permit-and-ban policy registry; cross-check every candidate here first

registries.name: brib/slopfree-software-index
registries.purpose: the ban catalog (~120 repos that reject AI code) — the negative list

projects.evidence: CONTRIBUTING.md §"Universal Agent Success Principles for Project Maintainers" plus .github/copilot-instructions.md, .github/chatmodes/, .github/instructions/ — deliberately structured for AI agents to contribute.

.github/copilot-instructions.md is a template-clone of moq.analyzers ('PRs that do not comply will be closed, regardless of author (human or Copilot)'). Same maintainer, same buildable analyzer scaffold — closest true sibling.
projects.note: Verify merge cadence: no merged PRs since 2025-06 though the maintainer is active on moq.analyzers.

projects.evidence: Root AGENTS.md + CLAUDE.md with detailed agent build/test/convention rules. Very active (weekly merges), small backlog, no CLA — strongest non-rjmurillo analyzer target.
AGENTS.md is an explicit agent-PR workflow contract ('open the PR only after build and tests pass'). Closest thing to an actual welcome; testing-domain adjacency to the anchor.
CONTRIBUTING.md §"AI-Assisted Contributions": welcome when they meet the same quality standards. A linter — closely analogous to the analyzer anchor.
Dedicated docs/about/contributing/AI_CONTRIBUTING.md — whole file is an AI-onboarding policy; welcome with mandatory manual verification.
Dedicated AI_POLICY.md: 'We welcome AI-assisted contributions from people who understand what they're submitting and are ready to collaborate on it.'
CONTRIBUTING.md §"AI Policy": AI-assisted contributions welcome as long as they solve a real problem, follow coding standards, and include tests. High issue volume.
CONTRIBUTING.md: AI-assisted contributions welcome, 'coordinated, scoped, and verified to keep review load manageable.' Top-star, but a stiff review bar.

avoid.note: Explicit AI bans or PR-restrictions — do NOT farm. AGENTS.md presence does not override a ban (libsdl-org/SDL ships one that bans). Full catalog in the slopfree registry above.
examples: dotnet/roslyn-analyzers (ARCHIVED — no PRs; moved to dotnet/sdk)
libsdl-org/SDL (AGENTS.md that bans AI PRs — the file≠welcome trap case)
